title: params.rs write non-atomic: partial file on process-kill during first-time download
description: params.rs:245 std::fs::write(path, &data) writes ~48MB non-atomically. Process-kill mid-write leaves partial file; next run fails BLAKE2b, deletes it, re-downloads. Self-heals but requires unexpected extra network round-trip. Fix: write to path.with_extension('tmp') first, verify hash, then std::fs::rename (atomic on POSIX same-filesystem).
